content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

I found one medium-severity correctness issue: the new lineage/detail queries source bead metadata from the current `plan.json` instead of the persisted plan provenance on the task/run, so historical task and run lineage becomes inaccurate after replans. `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, and `cargo fmt --check` passed during review; `nix build` was started but had not completed before I finalized this pass.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: Historical lineage is resolved from the live milestone plan instead of the plan hash/version captured on the task/run.
severity: medium
affected_files: ["src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Use the persisted `plan_hash`/`plan_version` (or persist a bead-title/acceptance snapshot) when filling lineage metadata, and fall back to durable IDs instead of projecting today's plan onto old runs/tasks.

Evidence: `load_task_lineage_detail` always reparses the current `plan.json` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1364-1426`), and `read_bead_lineage` / `bead_execution_history` / `list_tasks_for_milestone` do the same (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2065-2218`). Those lookups ignore provenance already stored on `TaskSource` and `TaskRunEntry` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs:25-41`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:364-376`). After any milestone replan or bead rename/removal, these queries will silently relabel historical tasks/runs with the current bead title and acceptance criteria, or return empty lineage details for runs that were valid under the earlier plan version.
   Rationale: The bead's goal is to expose milestone/bead lineage for tracing. Returning mutable current-plan metadata for historical runs breaks that provenance and can mislead both operators and controllers after routine plan updates.
